== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:八島牟遅能神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.

== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:八島牟遅能神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.

== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:八島牟遅能神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.

== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:八島牟遅能神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Notes == {{Help footnotes}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=October 30, 2014 |publisher={{ill|Shinchosha|ja|新潮社}} |ref={{SfnRef|Nishinomiya|2014}} |author={{ill|Kazutami Nishinomiya|ja|西宮一民}} (Editor) |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series={{ill|Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium|ja|新潮日本古典集成}} (New Edition)}}
== See Also == * {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} * {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}} - The first of the 17 generations.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/yashimamujinokami/ Yashimamujinokami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project]
{{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:17 Generations of Deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Deities of Japan]] [[ja:八島牟遅能神]]
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:八島牟遅能神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.

== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:八島牟遅能神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.

This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.

This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

[[ja:八島牟遅能神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.

This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Notes == {{Help footnotes}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=October 30, 2014 |publisher={{ill|Shinchosha|ja|新潮社}} |ref={{SfnRef|Nishinomiya|2014}} |author={{ill|Kazutami Nishinomiya|ja|西宮一民}} (Editor) |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series={{ill|Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium|ja|新潮日本古典集成}} (New Edition)}}
== See Also == * {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} * {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}} - The first of the 17 generations.

{{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:17 Generations of Deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Deities of Japan]] [[ja:八島牟遅能神]]
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:八島牟遅能神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:八島牟遅能神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.

This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.

== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:八島牟遅能神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.

== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = | Name = Yashimamujinokami | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 八島牟遅能神 (Yashimamujinokami) | Alternate Name= 八嶋牟遅能神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of the Land | Mausoleum = | Father = Unknown | Mother = | Relatives = | Consort = Unknown | Children = {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}} | Shrine = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Yashimamujinokami''' (八島牟遅能神, {{smaller|Modern kana orthography:}} ヤシマムジ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview == Yashimamujinokami is a deity mentioned exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is regarded as the parent of {{ill|Tottorijin|ja|鳥取神}}.
The name "Yashima" means "many islands," while "Muji" denotes "noble person." "No" is interpreted as a case particle, giving the name the meaning of "Noble person of many islands."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Yashimamujinokami's daughter is Tottorijin, who bore {{ill|Torinarumi no Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神}} with {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
